title: fragaria vesca subvar. muricata
intro: Fragaria vesca subvar. muricata, also known as Woodland Strawberry or Alpine Strawberry, is a subvariety of the wild strawberry plant that is native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which have been shown to soothe digestive issues and protect against cell damage. The health benefits of Fragaria vesca subvar. muricata include its ability to alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome, improve cognitive function, and support the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, tinctures, and infusions, and is also added to salves and creams for its potential skin-soothing properties.
